exercises.title: Override speak() in Animal Subclasses
exercises.description: Create a Notification base class with a send() method. Override it in EmailNotification and SMSNotification to add channel-specific behavior while calling super() to keep common logic.
exercises.expectedOutput:
[Notification] Sending to alice@example.com: Hello Alice!
[Email] Subject: Welcome to Python!

[Notification] Sending to Bob: Your code is 4521
[SMS] Sent to +1234567890
hints: def send(self): super().send() then the subclass-specific print
super().send() calls Notification.send() which prints the base message

exercises.title: Vehicle with Electric Override
exercises.description: Create a Vehicle hierarchy where ElectricVehicle overrides accelerate() and start() using super() to extend (not replace) the parent behavior.
exercises.starterCode:
class Vehicle:
    def __init__(self, make, model, year):
        self.make = make
        self.model = model
        self.year = year
        self.speed = 0

    def start(self):
        print(f"{self.make} {self.model} engine starting...")

    def accelerate(self, amount):
        self.speed += amount
        print(f"Speed: {self.speed} km/h")

    def describe(self):
        return f"{self.year} {self.make} {self.model}"


class ElectricVehicle(Vehicle):
    def __init__(self, make, model, year, battery_kwh):
        # TODO: Call super().__init__(make, model, year)
        # TODO: Set self.battery_kwh = battery_kwh
        # TODO: Set self.charge_level = 100.0
        pass

    def start(self):
        # TODO: Call super().start()
        # TODO: Print: "Battery at 100%"
        pass

    def accelerate(self, amount):
        # TODO: Call super().accelerate(amount)
        # TODO: Decrease charge_level by amount * 0.1
        # TODO: Print: "Battery now at 97.0%"
        pass

    def describe(self):
        # TODO: Call super().describe(), append " (Electric, 75kWh)"
        pass


tesla = ElectricVehicle("Tesla", "Model 3", 2024, 75)
tesla.start()
tesla.accelerate(30)
print(tesla.describe())
exercises.expectedOutput:
Tesla Model 3 engine starting...
Battery at 100%
Speed: 30 km/h
Battery now at 97.0%
2024 Tesla Model 3 (Electric, 75kWh)
hints: def accelerate(self, amount): super().accelerate(amount); self.charge_level -= amount * 0.1; print(...)
def describe(self): base = super().describe(); return f'{base} (Electric, {self.battery_kwh}kWh)'

exercises.title: MRO: Diamond Inheritance
exercises.description: Create a diamond inheritance hierarchy with A, B, C, D. Each class's hello() method calls super().hello() and prepends its own letter. Observe the MRO output.
hints: Python's MRO (C3 linearization) ensures each class is called once
[cls.__name__ for cls in D.__mro__] gives names only

warmup.title: Quick Recall: super() Behavior
warmup.description: What does this code print? Trace how super() chains through the hierarchy.
warmup.expectedOutput:
Hello from Base
Hello from Base and Child
